activities_projects.title: Lithospheric Plate Jigsaw
activities_projects.objective: Understand that the Earth's crust is broken into plates
steps: Paste a world map onto a piece of cardboard
Cut the map along the outlines of the major continents
Try to fit the coastlines of South America and Africa together like a puzzle
Observe how they seem to have been joined once
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that the Earth's surface moves and was once shaped differently
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful matching of continental edges (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Floating Paper Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Simulate how tectonic plates move on the mantle
steps: Fill the beaker with water and place small bits of paper on the surface
Heat the water gently and observe the movement of the paper
Note how the paper pieces move away as the water warms up
Compare this to plates moving on molten magma
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains that internal heat causes the movement of lithospheric plates
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the circular movement observed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Balloon Volcano Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand how a volcano erupts through a vent
steps: Put baking soda inside the bottle
Quickly pour vinegar and stretch a balloon over the bottle neck
Watch the gas (magma pressure) expand the balloon
Identify the 'Vent' and 'Crater' in your model
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the basic parts and cause of a volcanic eruption
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling of the vent and crater (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Earthquake Shake Table
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of vibrations on structures
steps: Fill a tray with sand and build a small tower of blocks on top
Gently tap the side of the tray (Focus)
Observe how the vibrations (waves) travel through the sand
See which parts of the tower fall first
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that earthquakes are vibrations that travel as waves from a focus
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation of wave movement and structural impact (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: River 'Meander' Drawing
activities_projects.objective: Identify features created by a river in the plains
steps: Draw a river flowing in a 'S' shape (Meander)
Draw a small curved lake separated from the river (Ox-bow lake)
Color the areas next to the river banks as 'Floodplains'
Label the 'Levees' (raised banks)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between different erosional and depositional river features
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling of Meander, Ox-bow lake, and Levee (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mushroom Rock Clay Art
steps: Mold a piece of clay into the shape of a mushroom (thin bottom, wide top)
Explain why the bottom part is thinner (wind erodes lower rocks more)
Make small piles of loose clay nearby to represent 'Sand Dunes'
Label the 'Mushroom Rock' and 'Sand Dune'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes how wind acts as an agent of erosion and deposition
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the mushroom shape and explanation of wind action (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sea Cave to Stack Sequence
activities_projects.objective: Trace the changes in rocks caused by sea waves
steps: Draw four boxes showing the stages of sea wave erosion
Box 1: Sea Cave (Hole in the rock)
Box 2: Sea Arch (Only the roof remains)
Box 3: Stack (Roof falls, only a wall is left)
Box 4: Sea Cliff (Steep vertical wall near the sea)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the continuous erosion process of coastal landforms
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical progression of drawings from cave to stack (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Glacial 'Moraine' Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Observe how glaciers carry and drop sediments
materials: Ice cubes with sand/pebbles frozen inside
steps: Place the 'dirty' ice cube at the top of the slanted tray
Let it slide down slowly or melt
Observe the pile of sand and pebbles left behind (Moraine)
Note how the ice 'carves' the surface as it moves
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that glaciers are 'rivers of ice' that deposit material
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the deposition left after melting (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Endogenic vs. Exogenic Flashcards
activities_projects.objective: Classify forces that change the Earth
steps: On one side of the card, write a force (e.g., Wind, Earthquake, Volcano, River)
On the back, write whether it is 'Endogenic' (Inside) or 'Exogenic' (Outside)
Quiz a partner to see if they can identify the source of the force
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Categorizes Earth's movements based on their origin
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in classifying the forces (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Earthquake Safety Poster
activities_projects.objective: Learn basic survival steps during an earthquake
steps: Draw a picture of a student hiding under a sturdy table
Write the three main spots: Safe Spot, Stay Away from, Be Prepared
List 'Drop, Cover, and Hold on' in bold letters
activities_projects.evaluation: Inclusion of correct safety zones and clear messaging (10 marks)
